- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_missing_default_template_is_loud_and_listed` — A defaults selection naming an absent template file is loud, listed (K8). [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]

- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_style_for_returns_defaults_when_unmapped` — A doc with no explicit mapping resolves to ``defaults``. [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_style_for_mapping_overrides_defaults` — A mapped doc resolves to its mapping, not defaults. [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_resolve_style_files_paths` — resolve_style_files maps each category to its on-disk template path. [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_read_style_guidance_contains_four_bodies_in_fixed_order` — All four bodies appear under category headers in doc-type→vocabulary order. [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_read_style_guidance_loud_on_missing_file` — read_style_guidance raises a loud ConfigError if a body file vanished (K8). [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_bundle_doc_style_none_when_absent` — No doc-style.yaml ⇒ bundle.doc_style is None (back-compat, K6). [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_bundle_loads_doc_style_when_present` — A present doc-style.yaml ⇒ bundle.doc_style is a populated DocStyleMap. [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `unit/test_docstyle.py::test_bundle_loud_when_doc_style_template_missing` — load_bundle surfaces the loud missing-template error from doc-style (K8). [`FEAT-QUALITY-001`, `FEAT-QUALITY-002`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]

### `unit/test_srcindex.py`

Module features: FEAT-REFERENCE-005, FEAT-REFERENCE-006

- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_build_returns_source_index` — build_source_index returns a SourceIndex of ModuleIndex records. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_modules_sorted_by_name` — Modules are sorted by module name; the re-export __init__ is skipped. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_public_symbols_only_and_sorted` — Each module carries its sorted PUBLIC symbol names (privates excluded). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_features_joined_by_module_name` — A module's features are the sorted catalog ids whose `modules` name it. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_module_path_is_repo_relative_posix` — A top-level module's path is its file; a subpackage's is its __init__/first. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_features_without_module_match` — A catalog feature naming a module absent from the package is reported. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_modules_without_feature` — A public module with zero catalog features is the orphan check. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_accessors_empty_when_reconciled` — With a catalog covering exactly the package modules, both lists are empty. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_build_is_deterministic` — Two builds of the same package + catalog are byte-identical (K10). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_build_loud_on_bad_root` — A missing package root raises a loud InventoryError (K8). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_records_are_frozen` — ModuleIndex / SourceIndex are immutable (frozen pydantic). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_moduleindex_forbids_extra` — ModuleIndex rejects an unknown field (extra=forbid). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_render_is_pure_and_stable` — Same index in → byte-identical markdown out (no clock — K10). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_render_lists_modules_symbols_and_features` — The wiki lists each module's path, public symbols, and feature links. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_render_coverage_summary` — The wiki carries a coverage summary naming any orphan module. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_render_coverage_summary_when_reconciled` — With zero orphans the summary says every module maps to a feature. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_real_tree_no_feature_names_missing_module` — Every catalog feature names a real module of the package (K8). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]
- `unit/test_srcindex.py::test_real_tree_no_orphan_public_module` — The deferred R-02 guarantee: every public module maps to a feature. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-005`, `FEAT-REFERENCE-006`]

### `unit/test_testwiki.py`

Module features: FEAT-REFERENCE-004

- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_finds_modules` — collect_tests returns one TestModule per test_*.py, sorted by path. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_finds_top_level_and_nested` — Both top-level and class-nested test_* functions are collected. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_nodeids` — Top-level → path::func; class-nested → path::Class::func. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_docstring_summary` — summary is the first line of the test docstring; '' when absent. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_boundary_from_path` — Boundary is resolved from the directory in the path. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_feature_inheritance` — Each case carries per-test + module-level Features, sorted + deduped. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_module_without_features` — A module with no Features: tag has empty module_features. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_boundary_unknown` — A test file outside the known boundary dirs → UNKNOWN. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_all_boundaries` — Each known boundary dir name resolves to its enum member. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_ignores_non_test_files` — Only test_*.py files are parsed; conftest/_helpers ignored. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_unparseable_is_loud` — A syntactically broken test file raises a loud CatalogError (K8). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_missing_root_is_empty` — A missing tests_root yields an empty tuple (no evidence, not an error). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_deterministic` — Two collections of the same tree are byte-identical (K10). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_records_frozen` — TestCase / TestModule are immutable (frozen pydantic). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_render_is_pure_and_stable` — Same modules in → byte-identical markdown out (no clock — K10). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_render_contains_boundary_headings` — The wiki has a heading per boundary present. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_render_contains_feature_index` — A per-feature 'tested by' index maps feature id → nodeids. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_render_includes_summaries` — Each case row carries its docstring summary. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_render_empty` — Rendering no modules still produces a valid (non-crashing) document. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]
- `unit/test_testwiki.py::test_collect_real_tree` — collect_tests over the real tests/ returns modules; every case has a [`FEAT-REFERENCE-004`]

### `unit/test_unit_serializer.py`

Module features: FEAT-CONFIGV2-014

- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_dump_round_trips_to_equal_model` — load_unit_file(write(dump_unit_file(u))) == u (full fidelity). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_dump_is_idempotent` — Dumping a loaded-then-dumped unit is byte-identical (K7). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_dump_refreshes_updated_field` — ``dump_unit_file`` writes ``now`` into the front-matter ``updated:`` field. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_upsert_document_replaces_in_place_and_appends` — upsert replaces an existing doc by id (keeping position) or appends a new one. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_add_and_remove_code_ref` — add_code_ref appends; remove_code_ref drops by path; both pure + round-trip. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_set_context_refs_replaces_wholesale` — set_context_refs swaps the whole list and round-trips. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_editors_loud_on_unknown_doc_id` — add/remove/set all raise a loud ConfigError for an unknown doc id (K8). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_remove_code_ref_loud_on_missing_path` — Removing a code_ref that is not present is a loud ConfigError (K8). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_set_context_refs_rejects_duplicate_path` — A duplicate path in the replacement set is rejected loudly (K8). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_set_document_owner_sets_partial_and_round_trips` — set_document_owner reassigns owner/team/dri; None leaves a field; round-trips. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`, `FEAT-OWNERSHIP-008`]
- `unit/test_unit_serializer.py::test_set_document_owner_loud_on_unknown_doc` — An unknown doc id is a loud ConfigError (K8). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-014`, `FEAT-OWNERSHIP-008`]

### `unit/test_wiki.py`

Module features: FEAT-REFERENCE-007

- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_wiki_targets_are_exactly_the_four_canonical_paths` — ``WIKI_TARGETS`` keys are exactly the four canonical wiki output paths. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_wiki_targets_render_to_nonempty_markdown` — Each render thunk produces non-empty markdown over the real repo root. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_committed_wikis_are_fresh` — ``regenerate(write=False)`` over the real repo reports nothing stale. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_regenerate_write_then_recheck_is_idempotent` — A first write produces all four; the on-disk render then re-checks fresh (K7). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_check_mode_lists_exactly_the_mutated_target_as_stale` — After mutating one wiki, check-mode lists exactly that file as stale (K8). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_missing_target_is_stale_in_check_mode` — A missing on-disk target reads as stale in check-mode (K8). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_results_are_sorted_by_path` — ``regenerate`` returns ``(path, flag)`` tuples sorted by path (K10). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_renders_are_deterministic` — Two renders of the same target over the same root are byte-identical (K10). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `unit/test_wiki.py::test_new_reference_feature_keeps_the_matrix_complete` — FEAT-REFERENCE-007 has a demo + a test, so the matrix stays complete. [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]

### `integration/test_agent_style.py`

Module features: FEAT-AGENT-004, FEAT-AGENT-005, FEAT-AGENT-006, FEAT-AGENT-008, FEAT-BACKENDS-008, FEAT-MONITOR-009, FEAT-QUALITY-003

- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_render_context_without_style_is_byte_identical` — No style_guidance ⇒ render_context is byte-identical to a None request (K6). [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_composed_prompt_includes_style_bodies` — The agent's compose node folds the guidance into the full prompt. [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_composed_prompt_without_style_omits_guidance` — No guidance ⇒ the composed prompt carries no writing-guidance header (K6). [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_monitor_style_guidance_only_for_no_renderer_llm_region` — _style_guidance_for fires for a no-renderer llm REGION; None otherwise. [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_monitor_no_doc_style_yields_no_guidance` — No DocStyleMap ⇒ _style_guidance_for is always None (additive, K6). [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_monitor_renderer_backed_llm_region_gets_no_guidance` — A renderer-backed llm region is rendered, not authored ⇒ no guidance. [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]
- `integration/test_agent_style.py::test_select_artifacts_unaffected_by_style` — Style guidance does not change which artifacts are selected (orthogonal). [`FEAT-AGENT-004`, `FEAT-AGENT-005`, `FEAT-AGENT-006`, `FEAT-AGENT-008`, `FEAT-BACKENDS-008`, `FEAT-MONITOR-009`, `FEAT-QUALITY-003`]

### `integration/test_config_index.py`

Module features: FEAT-CLI-002, FEAT-CONFIGV2-003, FEAT-CONFIGV2-004, FEAT-CONFIGV2-009

- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_on_disk_unit_not_in_index_is_loud` — A *.yaml unit on disk but absent from index.units → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_reverse_validation_names_all_offenders` — Multiple unindexed units are all named, deterministically sorted. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_reserved_stems_excluded_from_reverse_scan` — Reserved stems (index/ignore/doc-style) never count as missing units. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_indexed_units_in_sync_loads_clean` — The N-01 baseline (every on-disk unit indexed) still loads (no regression). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_sorts_units_alphabetically` — units: is rebuilt sorted by filename regardless of on-disk index order. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_includes_new_on_disk_unit` — A new on-disk unit absent from the index is added to the regenerated list. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_drops_stale_index_entry` — A unit listed in index but absent on disk is dropped from the rewrite. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_excludes_reserved_stems` — Reserved stems are never listed as units in the regenerated index. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_refreshes_updated_via_clock` — The frontmatter ``updated`` is refreshed via the injected clock seam (K10). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_preserves_globals_and_other_frontmatter` — Globals + frontmatter (except ``updated``) survive byte-for-byte. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_is_idempotent` — regenerate(write(regenerate)) == regenerate — a fixed point (K7). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_result_loads_back` — The regenerated text is a valid index that load_index_file accepts. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_empty_units_renders_empty_list` — No on-disk units → a ``units: []`` block (still a valid index shape). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_appends_units_block_when_absent` — An index body lacking a ``units:`` block gets one appended (insert path). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_inserts_updated_when_absent` — A frontmatter lacking ``updated:`` gets one inserted (insert path, K10). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_write_index_writes_bytes` — write_index materializes the text at config_dir/index.yaml. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_write_index_loud_on_oserror` — An unwritable target (a directory in the way) raises ConfigError (K8). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_integration_unindexed_then_regenerate_fixes` — 3 units on disk, one missing from index → load raises; regenerate fixes. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_rewrites_drifted_index` — `cdmon index` rewrites a drifted index to list every on-disk unit. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_second_run_is_noop` — A second `cdmon index` produces byte-identical output (K7). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_check_exits_1_on_drift` — `cdmon index --check` exits 1 when on-disk index differs (CI gate). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_check_exits_0_when_synced` — `cdmon index --check` exits 0 when in sync — even with a MOVED clock (N-06). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_default_config_dir` — With no --config-dir, the default config/cdmon under cwd is used. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_write_failure_is_loud` — A write failure during a non-check rewrite surfaces as a clean error. [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_regenerate_unreadable_index_is_loud` — An OSError reading the existing index → loud ConfigError (K8). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]
- `integration/test_config_index.py::test_cli_index_missing_dir_is_loud` — A config dir with no index.yaml → clean error, exit 1 (K8). [`FEAT-CLI-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-009`]

### `integration/test_config_v2.py`

Module features: FEAT-CONFIGV2-001, FEAT-CONFIGV2-002, FEAT-CONFIGV2-003, FEAT-CONFIGV2-004, FEAT-CONFIGV2-005, FEAT-CONFIGV2-008, FEAT-CONFIGV2-010

- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_split_frontmatter_empty_block_is_empty_mapping` — An empty fence is a valid empty mapping (not an error). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_split_frontmatter_comment_only_block_is_empty_mapping` — A comment-only fence (yaml -> None) normalizes to an empty mapping. - `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_split_frontmatter_missing_fence_is_loud` — No fence → loud ConfigError (a dir-layout file MUST carry one). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_split_frontmatter_malformed_yaml_is_loud` — A malformed YAML block raises ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_split_frontmatter_non_mapping_is_loud` — A non-mapping front matter (a list) raises ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_file_alias_round_trip` — Hyphenated YAML keys load into snake_case attrs (populate_by_name). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_index_file_alias_round_trip` — index.yaml hyphenated keys + defaults load correctly. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_bad_version_is_loud` — cdmon-config-version != 2.0.0 → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_index_bad_version_is_loud` — index frontmatter version != 2.0.0 → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_bad_extension_no_dot_is_loud` — A source-files-format entry without a leading dot → loud. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]

- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_empty_dir_covered_is_loud` — An empty dir-covered list → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_unknown_key_is_loud` — extra='forbid': a stray top-level key in a unit is loud. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_body_not_a_mapping_is_loud` — A unit body that is a list, not a mapping, is loud. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_load_config_dir_merges_documents_in_index_order` — documents = concat of unit docs in index order then in-file order. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_load_config_dir_globals_from_index` — version/root/backend/region_templates/coverage come from index.yaml. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_load_config_dir_equals_load_bundle_config` — load_config_dir(d) == load_bundle(d).config (the documented identity). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_missing_index_is_loud` — No index.yaml in the dir → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_indexed_unit_file_missing_is_loud` — A unit listed in index but absent on disk → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_duplicate_document_id_across_units_is_loud` — The same document id in two units → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_identical_dir_covered_across_units_is_loud` — Two units sharing an IDENTICAL dir-covered path → loud ConfigError (Z-01a). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_identical_dir_covered_normalized_spelling_is_loud` — An equivalent spelling (trailing slash / ./) of the same dir is still loud. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_nested_dir_covered_across_units_is_ok` — A parent + child dir-covered across two units now LOADS (Z-01a nesting). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_disjoint_dir_covered_is_ok` — Sibling dirs that share a prefix component but are disjoint are fine. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_bundle_unit_for_document` — unit_for_document maps a doc id back to its owning unit. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_for_path_child_file_attributes_to_child` — A file under the child dir attributes to the CHILD unit, not the parent. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_for_path_parent_file_attributes_to_parent` — A file directly in the parent dir (not under the child) → the PARENT unit. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_for_path_unrelated_is_none` — A file under no unit's dir-covered → None. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_for_path_sibling_prefix_not_nested` — Component-wise match: a file under ``.../agentry`` is NOT attributed to the [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_resolve_config_single_file_unchanged` — A single-file --config still loads via _resolve_config (back-compat). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_resolve_config_directory_uses_dir_loader` — Passing a directory uses the dir loader and returns it as config_dir. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_resolve_config_autodetect_when_missing_single_file` — No --config file present but config/cdmon/index.yaml exists → dir layout. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_resolve_config_explicit_file_wins_over_autodetect` — An existing explicit single file is honored even if a dir layout exists. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_whitespace_dir_covered_entry_is_loud` — A blank dir-covered entry → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_empty_source_files_format_is_loud` — An empty source-files-format list → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_empty_documents_is_loud` — An empty documents list → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_unreadable_file_is_loud` — A missing/unreadable file path → loud ConfigError on read. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_malformed_body_yaml_is_loud` — A malformed YAML body (after a valid fence) → loud ConfigError. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_empty_body_is_loud_on_required_fields` — An empty body (no required scope) → loud ConfigError (missing fields). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_unit_comment_only_body_normalizes_then_fails_on_fields` — A comment-only body (yaml -> None) becomes {} then fails validation. [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]
- `integration/test_config_v2.py::test_config_error_is_codedocmonitorerror` — ConfigError remains a CodeDocMonitorError so the CLI catches it (K8). [`FEAT-CONFIGV2-001`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-002`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-003`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-004`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-005`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-008`, `FEAT-CONFIGV2-010`]

### `system/test_wiki_cli.py`

Module features: FEAT-REFERENCE-007

- `system/test_wiki_cli.py::test_wiki_then_check_is_idempotent` — ``cdmon wiki`` then ``cdmon wiki --check`` both exit 0 (idempotent, K7). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `system/test_wiki_cli.py::test_wiki_run_twice_is_a_noop` — A second ``cdmon wiki`` reports every target unchanged (idempotent, K7). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
- `system/test_wiki_cli.py::test_check_fails_after_a_wiki_is_touched` — After appending a byte to a wiki, ``cdmon wiki --check`` exits nonzero (K8). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]

- `system/test_wiki_cli.py::test_committed_wikis_are_fresh_through_the_cli` — ``cdmon wiki --check`` exits 0 on the committed tree (no mutation). [`FEAT-REFERENCE-007`]
